evals.prompt: How should I manage state in an iOS app?
evals.expected_output: Use @Observable (iOS 17+) for modern SwiftUI. Use Combine with @Published for UIKit or broader compatibility. Expose state with UDF pattern: Input enum (events) and Output struct (state).

evals.prompt: What are the anti-patterns in iOS state management?
evals.expected_output: No uncleared subscriptions — use .store(in: &cancellables). No UI updates on background thread — use @MainActor or .receive(on: .main). No manual objectWillChange.send().

evals.prompt: How do I represent view state in iOS?
evals.expected_output: Use an exhaustive ViewState enum with cases: .loading, .success(data), .error(failure). This ensures all states are handled in the UI.

should_not_trigger: Set up a basic iOS project from scratch
How do I install iOS dependencies?
Configure CI/CD for an unrelated platform
